dependencies: Almost all raw and packaging materials purchased from third parties, some single-source suppliers
Fuel, natural gas and derivative products important for manufacturing and transportation
risks: Loss or impairment of key manufacturing sites
Discontinuity or disruptions in internal information and data systems or suppliers' systems
Inability to procure sufficient raw or input materials (including water, recycled materials)
Increasing severity or frequency of extreme weather events due to climate change
Cost fluctuations due to commodity price volatility

regulatory_risks: U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regulations
Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) regulations
Anti-corruption laws (U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act)
Privacy and data protection laws (EU General Data Protection Regulation, U.S. state regulations)
Human capital and human rights regulations
Income tax changes (2025 U.S. Tax Act, OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax)

sanction_risks: Sanctions and export controls related to Russia-Ukraine war
Restrictions on data transfers or access to financial institutions
Supply and transportation challenges due to geopolitical conflicts
Potential operational disruptions and asset impairments in sanctioned regions
Discriminatory or conflicting fiscal or trade policies in different countries
Changes to tariffs and existing trade policies and agreements
New or increased tariffs, quotas, exchange or price controls, trade barriers
Geopolitical tensions and trade controls disrupting business in key markets
Political instability, upheaval or acts of war
Sovereign risk related to default or deterioration in creditworthiness of local governments
